summary: TPP won all 15 seats in landslide. Recounts confirmed results. No legal challenge filed. PNM leader conceded and stepped down.
content:
Key facts:
- Election held January 12, 2026
- TPP won all 15 seats (clean sweep), with 60% of the vote (~16,240 votes vs PNM's ~10,456)
- PNM requested recounts in 2 districts (Darrel Spring/Whim and Scarborough/Mt Grace)
- EBC confirmed results unchanged after recounts on January 14
- PNM Tobago leader Ancil Dennis conceded and announced he would step down
- Chief Secretary Farley Augustine was sworn in on January 15
- PM Persad-Bissessar congratulated TPP and piloted THA Amendment Bill on January 16
- No evidence of any election petition or legal challenge being filed
- The result was a decisive landslide, not a close election
- Trinidad and Tobago has a functioning democratic system with independent EBC
- Historical precedent: In 2021, recounts also confirmed results with no change
